name: Campanario Reservoir Loop
name_original: De Valverde del Camino al Embalse de Campanario – circular por EuroVelo 1
description: Cycle a moderate 23.2 miles (37.3 km) circular route from Valverde del Camino to Embalse de Campanario via EuroVelo 1.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Valverde Del Camino traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and cork oak forests, forming part of the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park. The region features diverse ecosystems, including pine and eucalyptus forests, alongside the prominent Odiel River. Many routes utilize former railway lines, known as Vías Verdes, offering gentle gradients through scenic natural areas. This terrain provides varied cycling experiences, from flat, traffic-free paths to more challenging ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Valverde Del Camino The most popular touring cycling route is From Valverde del Camino to Minas de Riotinto via Riotinto Greenway, a 39.4 miles (63.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows an old railway line, showcasing the region's mining past. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Campanario Reservoir Loop, a moderate 23.2 miles (37.3 km) path. This route circles the Dique de Campanario, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding vegetation. Local touring cyclists also love the Transandalus and La Zarza Mine Loop, a 45.2 miles (72.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain with connections to mining heritage, often completed in about 4 hours 57 minutes. Touring cycling around Valverde Del Camino is defined by rolling hills, Vías Verdes (Greenways), and landscapes featuring olive groves and cork oak forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Valverde Del Camino are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 12 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Valverde Del Camino's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Valverde Del Camino?
A: Valverde Del Camino offers a diverse network of over 50 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy paths to more challenging rides, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Valverde Del Camino?
A: The terrain around Valverde Del Camino is characterized by rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and cork oak forests. Many routes utilize former railway lines, known as Vías Verdes, which offer gentle gradients and traffic-free cycling. You'll also find diverse ecosystems including pine and eucalyptus forests, and routes along the Odiel River. While some paths are flat, others feature more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Valverde Del Camino?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Valverde del Camino&lt;/a&gt;, which is a shorter, less demanding path ideal for a gentle introduction to the area's cycling. In total, there are 2 easy routes available.

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in Valverde Del Camino?
A: The Mediterranean climate of Valverde Del Camino makes spring and autumn the ideal seasons for touring cycling. During these months, temperatures are mild and pleasant, perfect for outdoor exploration. Summers can be hot and dry, while winters are mild and wet.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Valverde Del Camino?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984990875/campanario-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campanario Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that circles the Dique de Campanario, offering scenic views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Valverde Del Camino's routes often pass by significant natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5346190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Molino de Acha&lt;/a&gt;, a converted railway line, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge over the Odiel River&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calabazar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmens of Los Gabrieles&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the area's natural beauty and rich past.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Valverde Del Camino?
A: Many of the Vías Verdes (Greenways) are excellent for family cycling due to their gentle gradients and traffic-free nature. While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly categorized, the easier routes and sections of the Greenways provide a safe and enjoyable experience for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the Vías Verdes and natural trails in the region, provided they are kept on a leash and you clean up after them. However, it's always advisable to check specific regulations for protected natural areas or private lands you might encounter along a route.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Valverde Del Camino?
A: The touring cycling routes around Valverde Del Camino are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the historical mining heritage integrated into many routes, and the peaceful experience of riding on the Vías Verdes. The variety of routes, from easy loops to challenging climbs, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there routes that highlight the region's mining heritage?
A: Absolutely. Valverde Del Camino has a rich mining past, and many routes incorporate this history. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984125419/from-valverde-del-camino-to-minas-de-riotinto-via-riotinto-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Valverde del Camino to Minas de Riotinto via Riotinto Greenway&lt;/a&gt; follows an old railway line, showcasing the region's industrial legacy. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364132770/transandalus-and-la-zarza-mine-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus and La Zarza Mine Loop&lt;/a&gt;, also connects to mining heritage sites.

Q: Where can I find parking facilities for cycling routes in Valverde Del Camino?
A: Valverde Del Camino, being a hub for cycling, offers various parking options. You can typically find parking in the town center, near the old railway station (which is a historical site itself), or at designated trailheads for the Vías Verdes. Specific route starting points often have convenient parking access.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat along the touring cycling routes?
A: While some routes pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes and restaurants, many sections of the Vías Verdes traverse natural landscapes. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes. The town center of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5353024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valverde del Camino&lt;/a&gt; itself offers various dining options.
tips: Church from the 17th century, with the carving of the Virgen del Reposo, patron saint of Valverde since 1672.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular touring bicycle route is approximately 37.3 km long and generally takes around 2 hours and 55 minutes to complete at a steady pace. This duration can vary depending on your speed and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate in difficulty. While it's not overly challenging, it does involve some elevation changes (around 330 meters up and down), so a basic level of cycling fitness is recommended. Beginners with some experience should find it manageable.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Valverde del Camino. You should be able to find public parking within the town, close to the starting point. Look for designated parking areas or street parking options.

faqs.question: How can I reach the starting point of the route by public transport?
faqs.answer: Valverde del Camino is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services from larger nearby towns and cities. Check local bus schedules for routes connecting to Valverde del Camino to plan your journey to the starting point.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: For comfortable cycling, the best times to visit are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter in Andalusia.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay fees to cycle this route. It uses public roads and trails, including sections of the EuroVelo 1, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of cultural and natural sights. You'll pass by the historic Church of Our Lady of Reposo in Valverde del Camino and enjoy scenic views around the Campanario Reservoir.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While cycling with dogs can be enjoyable, it's important to consider the specific conditions. This route uses public paths and roads, so dogs are generally allowed. However, always keep your dog on a leash, especially near roads or in populated areas, and ensure they are comfortable with the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical for a touring bicycle route in the region. You can expect a mix of paved roads and possibly some well-maintained gravel or dirt paths, especially around the reservoir. The route has moderate ascents and descents, adding to the cycling experience.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other notable paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Transandalus, the Camino de Santiago del Sur, and various segments of the Vía Verde de los Molinos del Agua, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Valverde del Camino, where you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops for refreshments. While cycling between Valverde del Camino and the Embalse de Campanario, options might be limited, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks.
